description:
You are a script developer asked to demonstrate your proficiency with classic UNIX column-oriented
    text utilities (cut and paste).  
    The team lead has already placed two small CSV data sets in the directory
    /home/user/data.  
    1. /home/user/data/employee_master.csv  
       • This file is comma-separated.  
       • Column order: EmployeeID,FullName,Department,Role,JoinDate  
    2. /home/user/data/payroll_Q1.csv  
       • This file is comma-separated.  
       • Column order: EmployeeID,Jan,Feb,Mar  

    Your goal is to create a compact, tab-separated report that combines the identity
    information (EmployeeID and FullName) with first-quarter pay totals (Jan-Mar).

    FINAL DELIVERABLES (must exist when you finish):

    1. /home/user/output/employee_payroll_Q1.tsv  
       • Must be a plain-text, **tab-separated** file, no surrounding quotes anywhere.  
       • First row has an exact five-field header (case-sensitive):  
         EmployeeID<TAB>FullName<TAB>Jan<TAB>Feb<TAB>Mar  
       • Subsequent rows are sorted by EmployeeID (ascending, as they come from the
         source files).  
       • For each employee appearing in both source files, output exactly one line whose five
         fields come from:  
           – employee_master.csv → columns 1 and 2  
           – payroll_Q1.csv      → columns 2, 3 and 4  
       • Do NOT include employees that exist in only one of the two source files.  
       • A trailing newline is required at the end of the file.

    2. /home/user/output/process.log  
       • Plain text.  
       • Every command you run that affects the creation of the report must be recorded on
         a separate line in this log.  
       • Format for each entry (all on one line):  
         YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS<TAB>COMMAND  
         where COMMAND is the full shell command you executed.  
       • At least three log entries are expected (they may include intermediate checks,
         directory creations, etc.).  
       • A trailing newline is required at the end of the log.

    TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS & HINTS

    • You do NOT need root privileges; stay inside /home/user.  
    • Feel free to make temporary working files under /home/user/tmp but delete them or
      ignore them; the automated test will only inspect the two deliverables listed above.  
    • Only standard coreutils (cut, paste, sort, mkdir, etc.) are guaranteed to be present.
      Do not rely on csvkit or other external tools.  
    • Remember that cut counts fields starting from 1 and by default uses TAB as the
      delimiter; use the -d option for comma processing.  
    • The paste utility concatenates files line-by-line; therefore you may want to ensure
      both files are aligned and that you paste only the extracted columns, not the entire
      source lines.  
    • Because the test harness will verify exact file contents, double-check that:  
      – Tabs (not spaces) are used between fields.  
      – No extra header lines, blank lines, or trailing carriage returns are present.  
      – The final newline at the end of each file exists.

    Once everything matches the specification, you’re done.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing data files placed in the container before the task starts -->

    /home/user/data/employee_master.csv (mode 644)
    ------------------------------------------------
    EmployeeID,FullName,Department,Role,JoinDate
    1001,Ann Dowling,Engineering,DevOps,2015-06-11
    1002,Bob Tran,Sales,Account Manager,2018-03-17
    1003,Carla Méndez,HR,Recruiter,2019-11-05
    1004,Deepak Singh,Engineering,Backend Developer,2021-01-23
    1005,Eva Rossi,Finance,Accountant,2017-07-30

    /home/user/data/payroll_Q1.csv (mode 644)
    -----------------------------------------
    EmployeeID,Jan,Feb,Mar
    1001,12750,12900,12800
    1002,9450,9100,9300
    1003,8750,8800,8900
    1004,10600,10750,10850
    1006,9900,10050,10100

    Target files after successful completion:

    /home/user/output/employee_payroll_Q1.tsv (mode 644)
    -----------------------------------------------------
    EmployeeID\tFullName\tJan\tFeb\tMar\n
    1001\tAnn Dowling\t12750\t12900\t12800\n
    1002\tBob Tran\t9450\t9100\t9300\n
    1003\tCarla Méndez\t8750\t8800\t8900\n
    1004\tDeepak Singh\t10600\t10750\t10850\n

    Notes: employees 1005 (only in master) and 1006 (only in payroll) must be absent.

    /home/user/output/process.log (mode 644)
    ----------------------------------------
    • Must contain ≥3 lines, each matching regex:
      ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\t.*\n$
    • No specific commands enforced, but every line must be unique.
